speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: That which hath made them drunk hath made me bold.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: What hath quenched them hath given me fire. Hark!—Peace.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: It was the owl that shrieked, the fatal bellman,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Which gives the stern’st good-night. He is about it.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: The doors are open, and the surfeited grooms

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Do mock their charge with snores. I have drugged their possets,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: That death and nature do contend about them

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Whether they live or die.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Who’s there? what, ho!

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Alack, I am afraid they have awaked,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And ’tis not done. Th’ attempt and not the deed

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Confounds us. Hark!—I laid their daggers ready;

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: He could not miss ’em. Had he not resembled

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: My father as he slept, I had done ’t.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: My husband?

speaker: MACBETH
text: I have done the deed. Didst thou not hear a noise?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: I heard the owl scream and the crickets cry.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Did not you speak?

speaker: MACBETH
text: When?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Now.

speaker: MACBETH
text: As I descended?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Ay.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Hark!—Who lies i’ th’ second chamber?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Donalbain.

speaker: MACBETH
text: This is a sorry sight.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: A foolish thought, to say a sorry sight.

speaker: MACBETH
text: There’s one did laugh in ’s sleep, and one cried “Murder!”

speaker: MACBETH
text: That they did wake each other. I stood and heard them.

speaker: MACBETH
text: But they did say their prayers and addressed them

speaker: MACBETH
text: Again to sleep.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: There are two lodged together.

speaker: MACBETH
text: One cried “God bless us” and “Amen” the other,

speaker: MACBETH
text: As they had seen me with these hangman’s hands,

speaker: MACBETH
text: List’ning their fear. I could not say “Amen”

speaker: MACBETH
text: When they did say “God bless us.”

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Consider it not so deeply.

speaker: MACBETH
text: But wherefore could not I pronounce “Amen”?

speaker: MACBETH
text: I had most need of blessing, and “Amen”

speaker: MACBETH
text: Stuck in my throat.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: These deeds must not be thought

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: After these ways; so, it will make us mad.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Methought I heard a voice cry “Sleep no more!

speaker: MACBETH
text: Macbeth does murder sleep”—the innocent sleep,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Sleep that knits up the raveled sleave of care,

speaker: MACBETH
text: The death of each day’s life, sore labor’s bath,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Balm of hurt minds, great nature’s second course,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Chief nourisher in life’s feast.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: What do you mean?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Still it cried “Sleep no more!” to all the house.

speaker: MACBETH
text: “Glamis hath murdered sleep, and therefore Cawdor

speaker: MACBETH
text: Shall sleep no more. Macbeth shall sleep no more.”

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Who was it that thus cried? Why, worthy thane,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: You do unbend your noble strength to think

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: So brainsickly of things. Go get some water

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And wash this filthy witness from your hand.—

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Why did you bring these daggers from the place?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: They must lie there. Go, carry them and smear

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: The sleepy grooms with blood.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I’ll go no more.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I am afraid to think what I have done.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Look on ’t again I dare not.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Infirm of purpose!

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Give me the daggers. The sleeping and the dead

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Are but as pictures. ’Tis the eye of childhood

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: That fears a painted devil. If he do bleed,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: I’ll gild the faces of the grooms withal,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: For it must seem their guilt.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Whence is that knocking?

speaker: MACBETH
text: How is ’t with me when every noise appalls me?

speaker: MACBETH
text: What hands are here! Ha, they pluck out mine eyes.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Will all great Neptune’s ocean wash this blood

speaker: MACBETH
text: Clean from my hand? No, this my hand will rather

speaker: MACBETH
text: The multitudinous seas incarnadine,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Making the green one red.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: My hands are of your color, but I shame

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: To wear a heart so white.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: I hear a knocking

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: At the south entry. Retire we to our chamber.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: A little water clears us of this deed.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: How easy is it, then! Your constancy

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Hath left you unattended.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Hark, more knocking.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Get on your nightgown, lest occasion call us

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And show us to be watchers. Be not lost

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: So poorly in your thoughts.

speaker: MACBETH
text: To know my deed ’twere best not know myself.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Wake Duncan with thy knocking. I would thou couldst.
